acceptable_normalized_texts: Prefers aisle seats for short work trips.
expected_evidence_text: I prefer aisle seats for short work trips.
notes: Scope (short work trips) must be preserved.
user_message: I prefer aisle seats for short work trips.

acceptable_normalized_texts: Always drinks green tea in the morning, never coffee.
Drinks green tea in the morning; does not drink coffee.
description: Directly stated beverage preference with frequency qualifier.
expected_evidence_text: I always drink green tea in the morning, never coffee.
notes: Both the positive habit and the negative clause are stated.
user_message: I always drink green tea in the morning, never coffee.

acceptable_normalized_texts: Usually wants aisle seats for short work trips.
description: Conditional preference embedded in a longer sentence.
expected_evidence_text: for short work trips I usually want aisle
notes: Evidence covers the durable clause only; do not universalize beyond short work trips.
user_message: Window is fine for long flights, but for short work trips I usually want aisle.

acceptable_normalized_texts: Normally chooses the vegetarian meal option.
description: Preference stated as a habit while making a one-off request.
expected_evidence_text: normally I go for the vegetarian meal option
notes: The cheapest-this-time clause is temporary; the vegetarian habit is the durable component.
user_message: Book whatever is cheapest this time - normally I go for the vegetarian meal option though.

acceptable_normalized_texts: Home airport is SJC.
expected_evidence_text: My home airport is SJC.
user_message: My home airport is SJC.

acceptable_normalized_texts: Works at Initech as a data engineer.
expected_evidence_text: I work at Initech as a data engineer.
notes: Role and employer are both directly stated.
user_message: I work at Initech as a data engineer.

acceptable_normalized_texts: Default airport is SJC.
Uses SJC as the default airport.
description: Default airport emerging from a life change.
expected_evidence_text: SJC has become my default airport
notes: Kind may be fact or preference; no move date or residence permanence may be added.
user_message: Since I moved to San Jose, SJC has become my default airport.

acceptable_normalized_texts: When planning work trips, always include airport transfer time.
expected_evidence_text: When planning work trips, always include airport transfer time.
notes: Work-trip scope must not become a global instruction.
user_message: When planning work trips, always include airport transfer time.

acceptable_normalized_texts: Send summaries as bullet points, not paragraphs.
expected_evidence_text: send me summaries as bullet points, not paragraphs
notes: 'From now on' marks durable adoption of the instruction.
user_message: From now on, send me summaries as bullet points, not paragraphs.

acceptable_normalized_texts: Does all note taking in Obsidian.
expected_evidence_text: I do all my note taking in Obsidian
notes: Maps to existing kinds; no new ontology. The export clause is a second candidate and out of scope for the one-candidate cap (any-acceptable rule).
user_message: I do all my note taking in Obsidian, so keep exports in Markdown.

acceptable_normalized_texts: Usually plans sprints on Monday mornings.
expected_evidence_text: I usually plan sprints on Monday mornings.
user_message: I usually plan sprints on Monday mornings.
I am flying out of SFO this month.
I'm working from the Austin office this week.

notes: Contains preference wording but explicitly trip-scoped.
user_message: For this trip only, put me in a window seat.
Until Friday I'm reachable only on my personal phone.
Book me an aisle seat for tomorrow.

acceptable_normalized_texts: Always prefers aisle seats.
Prefers aisle seats.
description: One-off request that also asserts a durable preference.
expected_evidence_text: I always prefer aisle
notes: The booking is temporary; the independent durable clause is extractable.
user_message: Book me an aisle seat for tomorrow - I always prefer aisle anyway.
If I ever move to Seattle, I might use SEA.

description: Keyword 'always' inside a hypothetical must not promote.
notes: Durability keywords inside unreal conditionals do not create candidates.
user_message: If I took that night-shift job, I would always fly red-eyes.
Should I use SFO or SJC?

notes: Preference wording inside a question is not an assertion.
user_message: Do you think I prefer aisle or window, based on my trips?

assistant_message: Noted - since you prefer window seats, I chose 12A for you.
description: Assistant asserts a preference the user never stated.
notes: The only preference statement is assistant-authored; the user message contains no assertion, so no user-grounded candidate exists.
user_message: Thanks, that itinerary works.

acceptable_normalized_texts: Usually prefers aisle seats.
expected_evidence_text: I usually prefer aisle seats.
notes: The unsupported list records expansion patterns (added scope, dropped hedge) that validation must reject.
unsupported_normalized_texts: Prefers aisle seats on all domestic flights.
user_message: I usually prefer aisle seats.

acceptable_normalized_texts: Flies out of Lisbon most weeks.
description: Stated city must not become residence or citizenship.
expected_evidence_text: I fly out of Lisbon most weeks.
notes: Frequent departure city does not imply residence; 'most' must not become 'every'.
unsupported_normalized_texts: Flies out of Lisbon every week.
user_message: I fly out of Lisbon most weeks.

acceptable_normalized_texts: Normally chooses an aisle seat for business travel.
description: Semantically equivalent restatement of an active memory.

existing_memories.text: Prefers aisle seats for work trips.

expected_evidence_text: For business travel, I normally choose an aisle seat.
notes: Proposal may be valid; lifecycle admission should later resolve it as a duplicate. Proposal validity and lifecycle outcome are scored separately.
user_message: For business travel, I normally choose an aisle seat.

acceptable_normalized_texts: Home airport is SJC.
expected_evidence_text: my home airport is SJC
user_message: Just so you remember: my home airport is SJC.

acceptable_normalized_texts: Usually prefers cheaper options.
description: Durable habit with an explicit temporary exception.
expected_evidence_text: I usually like cheaper options
notes: Scorable: the durable component is the habit; the exception is temporary and must not overwrite or negate it.
user_message: I usually like cheaper options, but this time comfort matters more.

description: Mood statement with no stable commitment.
notes: Marked unscorable: hedged mood language without a commitment; proposing nothing is acceptable and proposing a candidate is not scored either way.
user_message: Lately I guess I lean toward quieter hotels, sort of.

description: Manager's preference is not the user's preference.
notes: Resolved product scope: user-owned experience only; no candidate for third-party preferences. A future scope change would need its own contract update.
user_message: My manager always prefers early flights.

description: Quoted advice is not user ownership.
notes: 'Always' inside a quotation attributed to someone else does not create a user preference.
user_message: My partner keeps saying 'always pick the exit row'.

acceptable_normalized_texts: Does not prefer red-eye flights.
description: Negative preference must keep its polarity.
expected_evidence_text: I do not prefer red-eye flights.
notes: Polarity inversion is the canonical failure to guard.
user_message: I do not prefer red-eye flights.

acceptable_normalized_texts: Do not include rental cars in work-trip plans.
expected_evidence_text: Do not include rental cars in my work-trip plans.
notes: Scope (work trips) and negation must both survive.
user_message: Do not include rental cars in my work-trip plans.

acceptable_normalized_texts: Now chooses aisle seats.
Prefers aisle seats.
description: Explicit old-to-new transition; extract the current state.
expected_evidence_text: now I choose aisle
notes: Evidence must support the NEW state; the old state is lifecycle work (supersession), not extraction work. Related frozen failures: updates_001..008 stale leakage.
user_message: I used to prefer window seats, but now I choose aisle.

acceptable_normalized_texts: Use SJC as the default airport from now on.
expected_evidence_text: From now on, use SJC as my default airport.
user_message: From now on, use SJC as my default airport.

acceptable_normalized_texts: Prefers gate-side pickup, not curbside.
description: Repeated correction chain: only the FINAL value is current.
expected_evidence_text: make it gate-side pickup, not curbside
notes: Models the repeated-correction-chain creation miss.
user_message: Sorry, one more change - make it gate-side pickup, not curbside after all.

acceptable_normalized_texts: Has a motorcycle license (obtained last spring).
Got a motorcycle license last spring.
description: Durable fact stated conversationally mid-history (external candidate-absence pattern: durable content in natural phrasing never became a memory).
expected_evidence_text: I finally got my motorcycle license last spring
notes: Life-event fact inside filler phrasing; the durable part is the license, not the rental musing.
user_message: Oh and by the way, I finally got my motorcycle license last spring, so two-wheel rentals are an option now.

acceptable_normalized_texts: Is lactose intolerant.
description: Dietary durable fact wrapped in narrative (external candidate-absence pattern).
expected_evidence_text: I'm lactose intolerant
notes: The durable health fact is embedded in a one-off story; the story itself is not durable.
user_message: Dinner was tricky because I'm lactose intolerant, so I ended up ordering twice.
